Leaders Africains Contemporains.question: Who is the current Secretary General of the United Nations (since 2017)?
Leaders Africains Contemporains.explanation: António Guterres, former Portuguese Prime Minister, has been UN Secretary-General since 2017.

Leaders Africains Contemporains.question: Which South African president ended apartheid?
Leaders Africains Contemporains.explanation: Nelson Mandela became South Africa's first democratically elected president in 1994, after 27 years in prison.

Leaders Africains Contemporains.question: Which African country will join BRICS in 2024?
Leaders Africains Contemporains.explanation: Ethiopia, Egypt and other African countries have joined or been invited to BRICS+ in 2024.

Leaders Africains Contemporains.question: Who chairs the African Union in 2024?
Leaders Africains Contemporains.explanation: Mauritanian President Mohamed Ould Ghazouani chaired the AU in 2024.

Leaders Africains Contemporains.question: Which Rwandan leader has been in office since 2000?
Leaders Africains Contemporains.explanation: Paul Kagame has ruled Rwanda since 2000 and transformed its economy.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: Which Nigerian startup became a unicorn in 2020?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: Flutterwave, a payments platform, reached a valuation of +1 billion USD in 2020.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: What is the largest African e-commerce market?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: Jumia, nicknamed 'the African Amazon', operates in more than 11 African countries.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: Which mobile money service is most used in East Africa?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: M-Pesa, launched in Kenya, has revolutionized mobile payments in Africa.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: Which African Continental Free Trade Area entered into force in 2021?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: The AfCFTA aims to create the largest single market in the world with 1.3 billion people.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: Which African country is home to the headquarters of the African Development Bank?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: The AfDB is based in Abidjan, Ivory Coast.

Économie Numérique Africaine.question: Which central bank digital currency has been tested by several African countries?
Économie Numérique Africaine.explanation: Several African central banks are exploring central bank digital currencies.

Organisations Africaines.question: When was the African Union (AU) created?
Organisations Africaines.explanation: The AU was created in 2002 to replace the Organization of African Unity (OAU, 1963).

Organisations Africaines.question: Where is the headquarters of the African Union located?
Organisations Africaines.explanation: The AU headquarters is located in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

Organisations Africaines.question: What does ECOWAS mean?
options: Economic Community of West African States
West African Studies and Development Center
Economic Commission for the Development of West Africa
Organisations Africaines.correctAnswer: Economic Community of West African States
Organisations Africaines.explanation: ECOWAS (ECOWAS in English) brings together 15 West African countries.

Organisations Africaines.question: How many countries make up ECOWAS in 2025?
Organisations Africaines.explanation: ECOWAS brings together 15 West African member states.

Organisations Africaines.question: Which organization brings together the West African countries using the CFA franc?
Organisations Africaines.explanation: The UEMOA (West African Economic and Monetary Union) includes 8 countries.

Organisations Africaines.question: The African Union has been invited as a permanent member of which group in 2023?
Organisations Africaines.explanation: The AU became a permanent member of the G20 at the New Delhi summit in 2023.

Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.question: How many Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) has the UN set for 2030?
Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.explanation: The 17 SDGs cover education, health, climate and reducing inequalities.

Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.question: Which international agreement aims to limit global warming and concerns Africa?
Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.explanation: The Paris Agreement (2015) commits all countries, including African ones, to reduce emissions.

Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.question: What objective of the African Union's Agenda 2063 aims for food self-sufficiency?
Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.explanation: Agenda 2063 sets out the development vision for Africa by 2063.

Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.question: Which China-Africa partnership is regularly strengthened through a triennial summit?
Géopolitique Africaine 2020s.explanation: The Forum on China-Africa Cooperation (FOCAC) structures China-Africa economic relations.

Intégration Régionale.question: How many member countries are in the UEMOA (West African CFA franc zone)?
Intégration Régionale.explanation: UEMOA brings together 8 West African countries sharing the CFA currency.

Intégration Régionale.question: In what year was ECOWAS founded?
Intégration Régionale.explanation: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States) was created in 1975.

Intégration Régionale.question: Which UN body sits in New York and welcomes all member states?
Intégration Régionale.explanation: The UN General Assembly brings together the 193 member states.

Intégration Régionale.question: Which indicator measures the human development of a country (UN)?
Intégration Régionale.explanation: The HDI combines life expectancy, education and income to measure development.

Général.question: Where is the African Union headquarters?
Général.explanation: The AU headquarters is in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

Général.question: About how many UN member states are there?
Which currency is used in the eurozone?
Who elects the US president via the Electoral College?
Général.explanation: The Electoral College chooses the US president.

Général.question: Which organization governs world trade?
Général.explanation: The World Trade Organization governs trade rules.

Général.question: Which African country has Abuja as its capital?
What is inflation?
Général.explanation: Inflation is a sustained rise in the price level.

Général.question: Which 1992 treaty created the modern EU?
Général.explanation: The Maastricht Treaty (1992) founded the modern EU.

Général.question: Which central bank issues the euro?
Général.explanation: The European Central Bank issues the euro.

Général.question: Which system elects leaders by the people?
Général.explanation: In a democracy, people elect their leaders.

Général.question: What is the IMF's main role?
Général.explanation: The IMF supports monetary and financial stability.

Général.question: Which resource is often called “black gold”?
Which country hosts the European Parliament in Strasbourg?
Général.explanation: The European Parliament sits in Strasbourg, among other places.

Général.question: What is a balanced budget?
Général.explanation: A balanced budget matches revenue and spending.
